close_reason: Decision: option (a) — error if cache is cold. Will be fixed in a version-bump PR.
description: In secretx-pkcs11/src/lib.rs, the SigningBackend::algorithm() method is synchronous (&self -> SigningAlgorithm) but performs HSM I/O (open session, find object, read CKA_KEY_TYPE/CKA_ECDSA_PARAMS) on a cold cache. This blocks the calling async thread. The method is documented with a warning, but callers discovering this at runtime — especially those calling algorithm() before sign() or public_key_der() have warmed the cache — will block their executor thread without any async boundary. The trait contract (synchronous return) makes a clean fix hard without a breaking change. Options: (a) return the cached value only and return an error variant if uncached, forcing callers to call sign()/public_key_der() first; (b) add an async prime() method to the trait; (c) accept the limitation and document it more prominently in the trait-level docs.
title: pkcs11: algorithm() blocks async executor on cold cache
